title: Parenting: MedlinePlus
content: Parenting On this page Basics Summary Start Here Learn More Related Issues Specifics See, Play and Learn No links available Research Clinical Trials Resources Find an Expert For You Teenagers Patient Handouts Summary If you're a parent, you get plenty of suggestions on how to raise your child. From experts to other parents, people are always ready to offer advice. Parenting tips, parents' survival guides, dos, don'ts, shoulds and shouldn'ts - new ones come out daily. The truth is there is more than one "right" way to be a good parent. Good parenting includes: Keeping your child safe Showing affection and listening to your child Providing order and consistency Setting and enforcing limits Spending time with your child Monitoring your child's friendships and activities Leading by example NIH: National Institute of Child Health and Human Development Start Here "Perfect" Parent Myth (American Academy of Pediatrics) Also in Spanish Nine Steps to More Effective Parenting (Nemours Foundation) Also in Spanish Positive Parenting: Building Healthy Relationships with Your Kids (National Institutes of Health) Related Issues Constantly Connected: How Media Use Can Affect Your Child (American Academy of Pediatrics) Delving Into Devices: Children and Screen Time (National Institutes of Health) Also in Spanish How to Talk with Kids about Tragedies and Other Traumatic News Events (American Academy of Pediatrics) Also in Spanish Kids and Media Use: How to Build Healthy Habits (Nemours Foundation) Shape Your Family's Habits: Helping Kids Make Healthy Choices (National Institutes of Health) Specifics Adoption and Foster Care (American Academy of Pediatrics) Also in Spanish Importance of Family Routines (American Academy of Pediatrics) Also in Spanish Parenting a Child Who Has Been Sexually Abused: A Guide for Foster and Adoptive Parents (Children's Bureau) - PDF Talk to Your Kids about Sex and Healthy Relationships (Office of Disease Prevention and Health Promotion) Also in Spanish Talk to Your Kids about Tobacco, Alcohol, and Drugs (Office of Disease Prevention and Health Promotion) Also in Spanish Talking to Kids About Race and Racism (Nemours Foundation) Also in Spanish Teaching Your Child Self-Control (Nemours Foundation) The Power of Peers: Who Influences Your Health? MedlinePlus also links to health information from non-government Web sites. See our disclaimer about external links and our quality guidelines .
